concept_decisions.retained_canonical_name: Surveillance system evaluation, monitoring, and reporting-quality studies
concept_decisions.labels_compared: surveillance_measurement_designs vs surveillance_case_finding_enhancement, underascertainment_burden_estimation, transmission_models_with_underascertainment, surveillance_and_burden_reviews, intervention_and_economic_decision_models, spatial_hidden_burden_models, bias_adjustment_and_risk_models
concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: Manifest JSON, concept table CSV, and all 150 titles plus complete available abstracts in the assignment CSV.
concept_decisions.rationale: The provisional bucket mixes distinct designs: surveillance audits/monitoring, case-finding tools, hidden-burden estimation, transmission models, reviews, causal/economic evaluations, spatial models, and bias-adjustment methods. The original label is retained only for papers whose main design is surveillance-system evaluation, reporting-quality assessment, or descriptive surveillance monitoring.

new_concepts.rationale: Covers active surveillance designs, reporting apps, NLP/ML detection systems, diagnostic decentralization, and other tools intended to increase capture of otherwise missed infectious events.
Covers studies whose main aim is to estimate true incidence, prevalence, deaths, or IFR after correcting for under-reporting, under-diagnosis, or incomplete mortality attribution.

Covers compartmental, state-space, and related epidemic-dynamics models that explicitly account for unreported infections, false negatives, or ascertainment variation when estimating spread or forecasting.

new_concepts.canonical_name: Evidence syntheses on surveillance gaps and hidden burden
new_concepts.rationale: Covers systematic reviews, meta-analyses, methodological reviews, expert overviews, and consensus reports whose main contribution is synthesis rather than new primary surveillance estimation.

new_concepts.canonical_name: Causal and economic evaluations under incomplete ascertainment
new_concepts.rationale: Covers intervention-effect studies, vaccination or NPI evaluations, benefit-risk analyses, cost-effectiveness analyses, and economic burden models where incomplete ascertainment materially affects decision analysis.

new_concepts.canonical_name: Spatial models of hidden burden and cryptic spread
new_concepts.rationale: Covers studies using spatial linkage, distance-based correction, niche mapping, mobility models, or geographically explicit outbreak prediction to infer missed burden or unobserved spread.

Covers methodological papers focused on correcting reporting bias, social-desirability bias, misclassification, spontaneous-report uncertainty, or other measurement distortions in inferred risks or associations.

concerns: ordered_key_sha256 was computed over the manifest ordered_candidate_keys joined with newline characters and no trailing newline.
PMID:32032971 and PMID:33313692 had no abstract text in the assignment CSV beyond the title/correction line; decisions used the complete available record content.
